NWS storm survey found high-end straight line wind damage from west of Highway 43 east to near Highway 59 in Bull City, including the Clearwater Estates, Four Winds, Vista Granda and Searles areas. Estimated 4 mile swath from north to south of extensive high-end wind damage with more sporadic wind damage observed up to 2 miles to the north and south of this corridor. Extensive high-end tree and utility infrastructure damage with minor to moderate structural damage observed. All damage was oriented in a linear west to east direction indicating straight line winds with the intensity of damage estimated to be caused by 90-100 mph winds which correlate well with KBMX radar velocity data.

A microburst occurred in the area of CR 40, CR 21, and CR 22 in the Tucker Crossroads community, caught on security camera. Several trees were downed, power poles were broken, and at least two homes had trees or power lines down on their roofs.
At the Lee County Farmers Market...large sections of metal roofing and trusses blown around. Large tree branches down.
Reports of wind damage in the Smiths Station area involving large limbs, a few trees, and power poles/lines. Time of occurrence is a couple minutes either side of 3:45 pm as downburst occurred.

Cleburne County EMA reports multiple impacts across the southern portion of the county with at least one bridge washed out, multiple roads are impassible due to flash flooding and there is a search and rescue operation underway.
A portion of Cherokee County Road 18 is washed out about 1.5 miles west of the intersection with Cherokee County Road 45. Road is impassable.
